stem: A surveyor stands 36 feet from the base of a building and measures the angle of elevation to the top of the building to be 53 degrees. Using the value tan 53° = 4/3, what is the height of the building, in feet?
explanation: In this right triangle, the distance from the surveyor to the building (36 feet) is the side adjacent to the 53° angle, and the building height is the opposite side. Using tan 53° = opposite/adjacent: height = 36 × (4/3) = 48 feet. This is a 3-4-5 Pythagorean triple scaled by 12.

distractorRationale.B: Divided the adjacent side by the tangent value instead of multiplying, computing 36 ÷ (4/3) = 36 × (3/4) = 27.
distractorRationale.C: Used the Pythagorean theorem with 36 and 48 to find the hypotenuse instead of finding the height, computing √(36² + 48²) = √(1296 + 2304) = √3600 = 60.
distractorRationale.D: Assumed the height equals the given distance, treating the triangle as isosceles: height = 36.
